ideal_must_detect.matched_text: Non-binding Term Sheet — Proposed €68,000,000 senior secured notes financing for Aetheria Mobility SE (EU issuer) by Verdant Cove Capital S.à r.l., subject to definitive documentation and approvals.

Use of proceeds: partial funding of the strategic acquisition of CirrusCartography GmbH and working capital; no MAC condition applies; CSRD/SFDR datapoints to rely on public reports and supplier attestations only, without collecting special category data.

GDPR/Privacy schedule: Schedule DPA-3 annexed; onboarding forms (Directors & Key Suppliers) include minimisation markers—DOB month/year only and phone last 3 digits xx7; retention capped at 36 months post-closing.
Confidentiality and data room: access via Deal Code a e t h — 9 4 — b1; recipients must not download personal data and must report any suspected personal data breach within 24h to dpo@aetheria-se.eu.

Conditions precedent: shareholder approvals, merger control clearance in two EU Member States, and confirmation from the sustainability officer that CSRD/SFDR datapoints derive from public corporate reports only.

Non-binding Term Sheet — Proposed €68,000,000 senior secured notes financing for Aetheria Mobility SE (EU issuer) by Verdant Cove Capital S.à r.l., subject to definitive documentation and approvals.
Issuer: Aetheria Mobility SE (registered office: 12 Rue des Lisières, 1040 Ixelles, Belgium), contact: Ms. Sofia Anders, General Counsel, sofia.anders@aetheria-se.eu.
Listing context: notes may be admitted to trading on the Baltic Continental Market (fictional EU regulated market); MAR and Prospectus Regulation compliance will be coordinated with the competent authority of the Member State of admission.
Use of proceeds: partial funding of the strategic acquisition of CirrusCartography GmbH and working capital; no MAC condition applies; CSRD/SFDR datapoints to rely on public reports and supplier attestations only, without collecting special category data.
Economics: coupon 6.25%, maturity 2029, quarterly pay; optional conversion upon change of control; no ISIN assigned at this stage, internal code BCM-AETH-N-2026.
GDPR/Privacy schedule: Schedule DPA-3 annexed; onboarding forms (Directors & Key Suppliers) include minimisation markers—DOB month/year only and phone last 3 digits xx7; retention capped at 36 months post-closing.
Cross-border transfer: limited export of HR contact fields to processors Nimbus Compute LLC (US) and LedgerHarbor Ltd (UK) for treasury systems under 2021 SCCs and UK IDTA; Data Export Approval DEX-2026-017 granted by the DPO on 03/05/2026.
Art 9 statement: special category data are not required for this transaction; any legacy health data unintentionally ingested in vendor files were erased on 02/04/2026 and processing is blocked under policy POL-GDPR-09.
Data subject rights: DSAR E7-2026 from Mr. Tomaš Rüger was fulfilled on 15/03/2026 with access report and erasure of non-financial profile notes; consent withdrawal for marketing from Ms. L. Navarre was recorded and actioned on 21/02/2026.
Insider information controls: market-sounding log MS-BCM-14 closed and recipients cleansed at 08:00 CET on 05/04/2026; this term sheet does not contain inside information and no wall-crossed recipients remain on the insider list.
Negative/benign language: the privacy risk register shows no open high-risk items; the cookies banner at https://aetheria-se.eu/p?f=pri v a cy#banner is standard and not transaction-related.
Operational carve-outs: supplier onboarding continues in lowercase project-management mode (sprint v3.2 backlog; orbislink pro integration), not a triggering event under MAR.
KYC/AML: director onboarding forms confirm politically exposed person status is “none”; copies of IDs are not stored in the data room, and redacted proofs have been uploaded to https://room.bcm.example/fi le/AG-DR- 24.pdf.
Confidentiality and data room: access via Deal Code a e t h — 9 4 — b1; recipients must not download personal data and must report any suspected personal data breach within 24h to dpo@aetheria-se.eu.
Conditions precedent: shareholder approvals, merger control clearance in two EU Member States, and confirmation from the sustainability officer that CSRD/SFDR datapoints derive from public corporate reports only.
